title: Unsafe OS Command Execution
description: Constructing OS commands from user-controlled input via subprocess, exec, or shell=True violates SOC2 CC6.1 and CC6.8 (change management). OS command injection can lead to full system compromise.

finding.message: OS command execution via '{{token}}' in {{file}} at line {{line}}. Verify input is not user-controlled.

references: Pass commands as array arguments, never as a shell string; use shell=False in Python.
Validate and whitelist all inputs used in command construction.
Use parameterized command APIs instead of string concatenation.
Consider sandboxing: run subprocess commands in a restricted container or with minimal permissions.
